title: Unique Paths in a Blocked Grid
description:
Given an m×n grid with obstacles where 1 indicates a blocked cell and 0 indicates free, count the number of unique paths from top-left to bottom-right moving only down or right. Return 0 if the destination is blocked.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ m, n ≤ 100
explanation:
Intuition
Use 2-D DP where dp[r][c] is the number of ways to reach cell (r,c). Blocked cells contribute zero. Transitions add from top and left if inside bounds.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Create dp with zeros; if start is blocked return 0
2. Main Algorithm: Fill dp row by row: dp[r][c]=0 if blocked else dp[r-1][c]+dp[r][c-1] with border checks
3. Return Result: Return dp[m-1][n-1]

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(mn)
- Space Complexity: O(mn) or O(n) with rolling row

Key Insights
- Borders need special handling since one parent may be missing
- Obstacles zero out paths downstream

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to seed dp[0][0]=1 when free
- Adding paths through blocked cells

testCases.explanation: Only path right then down is blocked, go down then right

hints: State depends on top and left neighbors
